results.title: Decomposing Representation Space into Interpretable Subspaces (2026)
results.snippet: LLM-side: NDM learns interpretable non-basis-aligned subspaces.
results.functional_justification: NDM partitions the representation space into interpretable subspaces — this directly implements the 'block-diagonal orthogonal projection partitions residual stream into N concept compartments' core leg of the candidate. While NDM is unsupervised (lacks the offline-authored cartoon routing and the adversarial soldering loss), the central operational primitive — learning a partition into named/interpretable subspaces of the residual stream — is functionally equivalent at the compartment-partition layer. Above 0.7.

verdict_rationale: Total hits = 2 (>= 1). Ranks 4 (NDM subspace decomposition, func=0.72) and 5 (subspace-constrained SAE, func=0.71) each pass the functional_equivalence>=0.7 leg of the OR rule, capturing the compartment-partition and compartment-bounded primitives of the candidate's LLM application. Rank 7 (Concept Layers) is explicitly acknowledged as prior art in the candidate's novelty_claim and scores 0.68 functional, just below threshold, so does not count, but the cartoon/soldering/closed-mesh distinguishing features remain unmet by any retrieved hit only because ranks 4 and 5 cover the partition primitive — the candidate's compartment-partition mechanism is not novel.
notes: Hits found through the functional_equivalence>=0.7 path on the partition/bounded-subspace primitive. The candidate's compartment-bounded subspace partition primitive is already implemented in NDM (2508.01916) and subspace-constrained SAE (2508.16929). The truly novel combination (adversarial soldering loss + offline-authored cartoon routing + closed-mesh integrity audit) is not retrieved, but the core LLM mechanism is anticipated.
